content:
1 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

We took on a Series A deck for an AI-guided, mobile neuromodulation platform built for dementia care. The thesis was credible and the clinical data was real. The problem was friction: bold colors, inconsistent type, dense slides with no visual hierarchy, and a go-to-market slide that didn’t exist yet.
The fix wasn’t more content. It was a system.
We built a clean design foundation, then used grounded AI imagery to show the device in actual clinical settings instead of speculative renders, so the product finally felt real on the slide. The problem slide moved from a wall of text to a clear view of dementia’s cost. We added the missing GTM slide and tightened traction and competitive copy down to what mattered.
The deck now walks an investor through a complex platform in minutes, and it supports the founder in the room instead of competing with them for attention.
See the before-and-after:
https://www.mideahub.com/portfolio-pages/series-a-pitch-deck-for-an-ai-driven-dementia-care-platform
